problem: Gate 22 required the lifecycle-state chain to exist as typed DMP surfaces before review and preview-PnL imports could continue honestly.
change: Added typed execution-lifecycle schemas and a Gate-22 contract service for the eleven lifecycle modules from dynamic_partial_exit_model through trade_logger; extended DMP imported-module payload support; added a short planning note and contract tests proving frozen order, truthful preview-state boundaries, and honest stressed-fixture degradation.
why: Freeze the lifecycle-state chain explicitly before widening into review, attribution, and preview-PnL contracts.
rollback: git revert the Gate 22 implementation commit
remove the execution-lifecycle contract schemas, service, tests, and planning note if reverting manually
re-run .venv/bin/python -m pytest -q, .venv/bin/python -m ruff check src tests, and .venv/bin/python -m mypy src tests

author: chatgpt
summary: Add Gate 23 review, preview-PnL, attribution, and variant-tracking contract surfaces
problem: Gate 23 required the review-chain and preview-ledger surfaces to exist as typed DMP contracts before the programme could truthfully stop at the Gate 24 planning decision point.
change: Added typed review-attribution schemas and a Gate-23 contract service for the nine review modules; extended DMP imported-module payload support; added a short planning note and contract tests proving frozen order, descriptive preview-ledger honesty, and stressed macro-caution/confidence-divergence handling.
why: Finish the requested three-gate execution-chain extension cleanly before stopping at the Gate 24 decision point.
rollback: git revert the Gate 23 implementation commit
remove the review-attribution contract schemas, service, tests, and planning note if reverting manually
re-run .venv/bin/python -m pytest -q, .venv/bin/python -m ruff check src tests, and .venv/bin/python -m mypy src tests

author: chatgpt
summary: Close Gate 27 as a planning reset and partition the remaining ready backlog across Gates 28–39

rollback: git revert the Gate 27 planning-reset commit
restore the prior gate map, scope note, leaf ledger, and execution log if rejecting the new Gate 28–39 plan

author: chatgpt
summary: Close Gate 39 review overlays and feedback-chain tranche
problem: Gate 39 remained as the final six-item remaining-ready tranche after Gate 38, blocking truthful completion of the current contract-import programme.
change: Added a deterministic Gate-39 coverage test and a reusable support-output bundle so the existing review overlay and advisory hedge surfaces can be proven in the exact frozen six-item order; updated the planning quartet to mark Gate 39 complete and the remaining-ready backlog exhausted.

author: chatgpt
summary: Close Gates 41-44 for raw/derived workbook split, options-first playbook expansion, and DMP audit
problem: After Gate 40 the repo still lacked an authoritative raw-versus-derived signal workbook split, an explicit same-bucket volume baseline policy, and options-first named playbooks that could use the richer options surface without destabilising DMP.
change: Added Gate 40-44 planning receipts, copied the v3 signal workbook into docs/planning, extended the checked-in playbook registry with front_expiry_pin_pressure, term_structure_dislocation, and skew_pressure_reversal, added deterministic eligibility evaluators and focused runtime tests for those playbooks, updated registry fixtures, and fixed the mypy-clean temporal loader/test surfaces while keeping DMP payload identity stable.
why: Finish the immediate temporal/options tranche cleanly so the repo has truthful raw-data doctrine, explicit options-first playbooks, and a green verification surface before the next downstream gate.
rollback: git revert the Gates 41-44 expansion commit
remove the three new playbooks from the checked-in registry and restore the prior planning quartet if reverting manually

author: chatgpt
summary: Install the Gate 46-50 planning pack and freeze the pre-implementation audit on a planning branch
problem: After Gate 44 the repo still had only a vague Gate 45 placeholder and no canonical leaf pack for the next audited architecture tranche.
change: Imported the pre-implementation audit into docs/audit, wrote Gate 46 through Gate 50 planning notes, updated PLANS plus the planning quartet to retire Gate 45 and install the bounded Gate 46-50 pack, extended the leaf ledger with 22 planned leaves, and added a narrow planning-integrity test for the new pack.
why: Pin the next architecture tranche before implementation so registry-v2, carry-handoff, temporal compatibility, and vocabulary governance work proceed under bounded gate control.
rollback: remove the Gate 46-50 planning notes and audit folder if the pack is rejected
restore PLANS and the planning quartet to the Gate 44 / Gate 45 placeholder state if reverting manually

author: chatgpt
summary: Close Gates 47-50 for registry v2, carry-horizon handoff, temporal compatibility, and vocabulary governance
problem: After the Gate 46 audit/planning freeze, the repo still needed the flat playbook registry replaced, the carry-handoff contract formalised, the outward temporal naming policy made explicit, and vocabulary governance rebased onto current architecture.
change: Implemented registry v2 with family/setup-variant/execution-expression/horizon hierarchy, threaded close-state handoff into carry services and replay, added outward temporal_state surfaces while keeping session_clock as an explicit compatibility wrapper, and introduced a canonical vocabulary schema/file/workflow with enforcement tests; updated the planning quartet and gate notes to mark Gates 46-50 complete on the execution branch.
why: Finish the audited architecture tranche cleanly before any downstream gate is authored.
rollback: git revert the Gates 47-50 execution commit
restore the planning quartet, registry surfaces, carry handoff, temporal compatibility surfaces, and vocabulary files if reverting manually

author: chatgpt
summary: Add anti-drift closeout protocol and recover missing Gate 59-64 execution receipts
problem: Audit review found that Gates 59-64 were materially implemented but the planning/status surfaces were not fully self-consistent: PLANS still mentioned Gate 61 in one place and the execution log stopped at Gate 58.
change: Updated PLANS and AGENTS with explicit anti-drift closeout rules, repaired the stale Gate 61 wording to Gate 64, reconstructed truthful execution-log receipt recovery for Gates 59-64 from the merged commits, and added a dedicated successor-pack anti-drift test so future closeouts must keep PLANS, the gate map, the active leaf ledger, and the execution log aligned.
why: Prevent status drift before Gate 65 and make future gate closeout failures visible immediately.
rollback: git revert the anti-drift hardening commit
remove the receipt-recovery block and anti-drift test if reverting manually
restore PLANS and AGENTS to the prior wording if reverting manually
